acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_award_ending_overexplains] Pass if a cold reader can identify one reveal beat anchored by Mort's entry and the meeting note at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:441 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:459, one relational beat with Ray at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:483 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:521, and one final closing image/consequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:541 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:543, without encountering additional paragraphs that merely restate that bureaucratic violence looks ordinary. Fail if the revised ending still spends multiple separate paragraphs paraphrasing the same thesis after the reveal has already landed.

[chapter_04_craft_timeline_arithmetic] Pass if the revised opening and week structure can be read straight through with no date math problem: the number of missed workdays stated at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:3 must reconcile with the Monday return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:5 and the Friday Eastbridge review at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:407. Fail if the chapter still implies six missed workdays before that Friday without establishing where the extra day occurred.

[chapter_04_prose_formula_tics] Pass if no more than two of the cited beats still rely on the same 'not X' contrast template or an abstract thesis sentence, and the revised passages instead show distinct sentence movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:303, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:441, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:519, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:527. Fail if three or more of those moments still arrive in near-identical contrast-formula language.

[F09] Chapter 4 (lines 926-1470) must contain: (1) no more than 1 instance of 'the kind of [noun] that'; (2) no more than 1 instance of 'not because... but because' (or 'not because... because'); (3) the remaining instances must be replaced with varied constructions, not simply deleted — the information they carry should still be conveyed.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can identify one reveal beat anchored by Mort's entry and the meeting note at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:441 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:459, one relational beat with Ray at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:483 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:521, and one final closing image/consequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:541 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:543, without encountering additional paragraphs that merely restate that bureaucratic violence looks ordinary. Fail if the revised ending still spends multiple separate paragraphs paraphrasing the same thesis after the reveal has already landed.
findings.problem: The chapter's real turn lands when Nate sees Morton's formal name, the demolition schedule, and Dominic's note about meeting Mort and Linda after hours. After that, the closing movement keeps restating the same bureaucratic-banality thesis in fresh wording instead of trusting the already-earned material. The result is a softer final blow than the chapter's strongest images deserve, which keeps the chapter below award-level compression and decisiveness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or re-sequence the closing movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:465, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:525, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:541 so it does three jobs only once each: the file reveals what is happening to Mort, Ray's brief return confirms how ordinary the office thinks this work is, and the chapter closes on Nate's guilt at occupying Dominic's chair. Keep the Eastbridge reveal and the Ray beat, but stop re-diagnosing the same insight after it is already legible on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised opening and week structure can be read straight through with no date math problem: the number of missed workdays stated at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:3 must reconcile with the Monday return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:5 and the Friday Eastbridge review at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:407. Fail if the chapter still implies six missed workdays before that Friday without establishing where the extra day occurred.
findings.problem: The opening says Nate spent the first six workdays of Dominic's life calling in sick, but this chapter's own Monday-through-Friday structure and the continuity sheet's canonical July 22 Eastbridge discovery do not leave room for six missed workdays after the July 11 transfer. As written, the chapter creates a timeline arithmetic contradiction at the exact point where the workweek framing is supposed to orient the reader.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the temporal count at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:3 so it matches the Monday office return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:5 and the Friday Eastbridge status pass at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:407. The cleanest repair is to change the sick-leave count to five workdays, unless you explicitly establish an additional missed workday on-page before the Monday return.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than two of the cited beats still rely on the same 'not X' contrast template or an abstract thesis sentence, and the revised passages instead show distinct sentence movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:303, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:441, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:519, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:527. Fail if three or more of those moments still arrive in near-identical contrast-formula language.
findings.problem: Several key beats default to the same polished contrast scaffolds: 'not X exactly,' the stacked 'Not Dad. Not Mort.' negation, and abstract summative lines like the bureaucracy 'miracle' sentence. Used once, these are choices; repeated at multiple pressure points, they start to feel like a house formula. That repetition makes the prose sound composed around the moment instead of fully inside Nate's raw processing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the high-pressure beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:303, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:441, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:443, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:519, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_04.md:527 so at least half of them land through distinct concrete observation, bodily response, or office detail rather than recycled contrast syntax or quotable abstraction. Preserve the pressure; vary the sentence shapes.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 4 (lines 926-1470) must contain: (1) no more than 1 instance of 'the kind of [noun] that'; (2) no more than 1 instance of 'not because... but because' (or 'not because... because'); (3) the remaining instances must be replaced with varied constructions, not simply deleted — the information they carry should still be conveyed.
findings.problem: Prose-formula density. Chapter 4 contains five instances of 'the kind of [noun] that' (lines 1128, 1320, 1368 [doubled in one paragraph], and two more nearby) and three instances of 'not because X but because Y' within 545 lines. Both constructions do identical rhetorical work — they block the reader's assumed interpretation and substitute a more precise one — and their co-occurrence within a single chapter creates a detectable explanatory rhythm. 'The kind of' appears 29 times across 14 chapters novel-wide; Chapter 4 is the densest at five instances. 'Not because...but because' appears 37 times across 16 chapters.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md lines 926-1470 (Chapter 4): reduce 'the kind of [noun] that' from 5 instances to at most 1. Keep the strongest or most specific instance; replace the others with direct description that does not use the 'the kind of' template. For the doubled instance at line 1368 ('The kind of notation a person made between cups of coffee. The kind of notation that could destroy a life'), keep one or rewrite both into a single sentence that conveys the same irony without the template. Similarly, reduce 'not because X but because Y' from 3 to at most 1 by varying the explanatory move — e.g., simple declarative, or the unexplained statement that trusts the reader to infer.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central premise involves consciousness murder, identity theft, sexual fraud, and the demolition of a working-class community for profit. It must not flinch from any of these. The sex scenes between Nate and Sarah must reckon with the fact that Sarah is being deceived about who is in the bed with her — this is not a comfortable situation and the prose should not make it comfortable. The grief scenes must be physically specific — no 'he was devastated,' only the concrete gestures and sensory details of a man watching his life's work get torn down. The guilt should live in Nate's body (Dominic's body), not in tidy moral reflections. When the novel is funny, it should be funny about things that are actually terrible — the comedy should never feel safe. When Nate discovers that Dominic was trying to help, the reader should feel sick, not enlightened. Risk specificity over tastefulness at every turn.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in place of specificity is a craft failure. When a scene requires difficult content — the moral weight of having killed an innocent man, the complicated ethics of sex under false identity, the physical reality of a man losing his livelihood, the specific humiliation of professional incompetence, the material texture of gambling debts and their enforcement — render it with the same care and specificity given to any other scene. The reader should feel the roughness of the situation, not a smoothed-over summary of it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Victor's professional register when delivering serious messages. If a line reads as stiff or literary, check whether contractions would make it sound spoken.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter energy aggressively. A comic workplace chapter should not be followed by another comic workplace chapter. A grief-heavy scene should be followed by something that lets the reader breathe — not through tonal whiplash but through a genuine shift in the kind of pressure being applied. Vary scene counts, dialogue-to-narration ratios, and temporal compression. Some chapters should cover hours; others should cover weeks. At least three chapters should contain extended quiet: meals, walks, conversations about nothing important. These are not filler — they are the human baseline that makes the high-pressure scenes land.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain American English with a slightly elevated attention to physical specificity. Not minimalist — the prose has room for interiority and texture — but never ornate. Latinate vocabulary only when the concept demands it. Prefer the concrete noun over the abstract one, the specific tool name over the generic 'tool,' the actual menu item over 'food.' The prose should feel like a smart person thinking clearly, not a writer crafting sentences.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, limited to Nate. The camera sits inside Nate's skull — we see through his eyes, think in his rhythms, and register the world through his specific sensory and emotional biases. The narration should reflect Nate's intelligence and his limitations simultaneously: he notices things (sharp observer) but misinterprets them (blinded by resentment, guilt, and self-pity). The narrative should not explain things Nate wouldn't articulate and should not achieve insights Nate hasn't earned.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied sentence length with a center of gravity around 15-20 words. Short declaratives for landing beats. Longer, accumulative sentences for sensory description and interior processing. Avoid sustained staccato (more than three short sentences in sequence) and sustained lyricism (more than two long, clause-heavy sentences in sequence). The prose should breathe.
